title: BOM Decomposition — NAICS 334413 Semiconductor and Related Device Manufacturing

sections.title: NAICS Routing
sections.content:
| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| NAICS sector | 33 (Manufacturing) |
| Product BOM applies? | **YES** — end product is a physical device with compositional structure |
| BOMs required | **BOM 1** end product (semiconductor device); **BOM 2** VN system where Mein Solar is a material input (Fab Power-Quality & Ride-Through System) |

**Rule applied:** Mein Solar's product is a material input to BOM 2 only (a system inside the VN at the fab facility level). It is NOT a material input to the chip itself. BOM 1 is produced for completeness; Mein Solar carries **no position** inside it.

---

sections.title: BOM 1 — Semiconductor Device (End Product)
sections.content:
Mein Solar is NOT positioned in this BOM. Included for CFJ traceability and adjacency completeness only. Kept compact.

### B.1 L4 Subsystems

| L4 Subsystem | Function |
|---|---|
| Active device region | Transistors, capacitors, diodes — core logic/memory function |
| Interconnect stack (BEOL) | Multi-layer metal wiring connecting devices |
| Substrate | Silicon wafer carrying the device layers |
| Package / encapsulation | Containment, thermal/mechanical protection, signal I/O |
| Die attach & bond interconnect | Electrical + mechanical bond to package |

### B.2 L4→L0 Decomposition (compact)

```
L4 Active device region
  L3 FEOL transistor layer
    L2 Gate stack assembly
      L1 Gate electrode (polysilicon or metal gate)
        L0 Polysilicon / TiN / W
      L1 High-k dielectric
        L0 HfO2
      L1 Gate spacer
        L0 Si3N4
    L2 Source/drain region
      L1 Doped silicon region
        L0 Silicon (epi) + B/P/As dopants
L4 Interconnect stack (BEOL)
  L3 Metal wiring layer (M1–Mn)
    L2 Damascene line assembly
      L1 Metal line  →  L0 Copper
      L1 Barrier liner  →  L0 TaN / Co
      L1 ILD (low-k)  →  L0 Carbon-doped SiO2 / SiCOH
L4 Substrate
  L3 Wafer
    L2 Bulk wafer  →  L1 Monocrystalline wafer  →  L0 Electronic-grade silicon (9N)
    L2 Epitaxial layer  →  L1 Epi film  →  L0 SiGe / Si epi
L4 Package / encapsulation
  L3 Substrate laminate
    L2 Build-up layer  →  L1 Dielectric film  →  L0 Epoxy / ABF
    L2 Trace layer  →  L1 Copper trace  →  L0 Copper
  L3 Mold compound
    L2 Overmold  →  L1 EMC block  →  L0 Silica-filled epoxy
  L3 Lid/heat spreader (advanced nodes)
    L2 Lid assembly  →  L1 Machined lid  →  L0 Nickel-plated copper
L4 Die attach & bond
  L3 Die attach  →  L2 DAF film  →  L1 Adhesive film  →  L0 Ag-filled epoxy
  L3 First-level interconnect
    L2 Flip-chip bump array  →  L1 Solder bump  →  L0 SnAg / SnAgCu
    L2 Wire bond (legacy)  →  L1 Bond wire  →  L0 Au / Cu / Ag-alloy
```

### B.3 Key L0 Alternatives (core process choice points)

**L4 Active device region → L3 Gate stack — Gate electrode alternatives (sub-5nm logic, 2026 share est.)**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| TiN metal gate (HKMG) | 85% | → Stable | HIGH | IEEE IEDM proceedings, foundry PDKs |
| Polysilicon (legacy ≥28 nm) | 13% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | industry analyst estimate |
| W / Co metal gate (emerging GAA) | 2% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | analyst estimate |

**L4 Interconnect → L3 Metal wiring — Line metal alternatives**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Copper (damascene) | 88% | → Stable | HIGH | IMEC, IEDM |
| Cobalt (sub-10 nm local lines) | 8% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | IEDM proceedings |
| Ruthenium (emerging sub-3 nm) | 3% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | IMEC roadmap |
| Aluminum (legacy ≥90 nm) | 1% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | single-source: false (still shipping in legacy fabs) |

**L4 Substrate → L0 Wafer material**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Monocrystalline Si (300 mm) | 80% | → Stable | HIGH | SEMI Silicon Manufacturers Group |
| Monocrystalline Si (200 mm) | 14% | → Stable | HIGH | SEMI |
| SOI (silicon-on-insulator) | 3% | → Stable | HIGH | Soitec reports |
| SiC (power/compound) | 2% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Yole Développement |
| GaN-on-Si (RF/power) | 1% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Yole |

(Remaining L0 commodities — silica, HfO2, EMC, SnAg, etc. — abbreviated here; each carries a single-source or near-single-source designation and would be elaborated in a full fab-level study.)

---

sections.title: BOM 2 — Fab Power-Quality & Ride-Through System (VN System — Mein Solar positioned)
sections.content:
This is the BOM where Mein Solar's product is a material input. The "system" sits at VN L5 (fab facility power infrastructure). Below it, the BOM runs L4→L0.

### B.1 L4 Subsystems

| L4 Subsystem | Function |
|---|---|
| Main power distribution | Step-down from utility feeder to fab MV/LV buses |
| **Ride-through / energy reserve** | Hold the fab tool load through sags (SEMI F47: 50% for 200 ms, 70% for 500 ms, 80% for 1 s) and short outages ← **MEIN SOLAR POSITION** |
| Power conditioning | Voltage regulation, harmonic filtering, reactive compensation |
| Control & monitoring | SCADA, power-quality metering, alarming, load shedding |
| Fire-protection & containment | Suppression, venting, compartmentalization (esp. for Li-ion) |

### B.2 L4→L0 Decomposition

Full decomposition of the ride-through subsystem where Mein Solar is positioned. Other L4s compacted.

```
L4 Ride-through / energy reserve   ← MEIN SOLAR position (as L3 Li-ion BESS module supplier via EPC)
  L3 Ride-through asset                              ← primary alternative choice point
  |
  +-- Option A: Flywheel UPS module (dominant in fabs today)
  |     L2 Rotor-motor assembly
  |       L1 Flywheel rotor                →  L0 High-strength steel OR carbon-fiber composite
  |       L1 Motor-generator              →  L0 Electrical steel laminations + Cu windings
  |       L1 Magnetic bearing             →  L0 NdFeB magnet + steel
  |     L2 Vacuum housing                  →  L1 Enclosure  →  L0 Carbon steel + seals
  |     L2 PCS inverter                    →  L1 IGBT module  →  L0 Silicon + AlN ceramic + Cu baseplate
  |
  +-- Option B: Li-ion BESS module (Mein Solar delivers this via EPC)   ← COMPANY POSITION
  |     L2 Battery rack assembly
  |       L1 Battery cell (LFP prismatic)
  |         L0 LFP cathode (LiFePO4)
  |         L0 Graphite anode
  |         L0 Electrolyte (LiPF6 in EC/DMC)
  |         L0 Al cathode current collector
  |         L0 Cu anode current collector
  |         L0 Polyolefin separator
  |       L1 BMS PCB
  |         L0 FR4 laminate + Si ICs + Sn-Ag solder
  |       L1 Busbar  →  L0 Copper (tin-plated)
  |     L2 Rack frame  →  L1 Frame rail  →  L0 Galvanized structural steel
  |     L2 Thermal management
  |       L1 Coolant pump  →  L0 Cast aluminum + rotor steel
  |       L1 Heat exchanger  →  L0 Aluminum + copper
  |       L1 Coolant charge  →  L0 Ethylene glycol + water
  |     L2 Container envelope
  |       L1 ISO 20ft/40ft HC shell  →  L0 Corten steel
  |       L1 Insulation  →  L0 Mineral wool
  |     L2 PCS inverter (bidirectional 4-quadrant)
  |       L1 IGBT / SiC MOSFET module  →  L0 Si or SiC + AlN + Cu
  |       L1 DC-link capacitor  →  L0 Metalized PP film + Al can
  |       L1 AC/DC inductor  →  L0 Si-steel laminations + Cu
  |     L2 Fire-suppression assembly
  |       L1 Detection head  →  L0 Photoelectric Si sensor
  |       L1 Agent cylinder  →  L0 NOVEC 1230 / FK-5-1-12 OR aerosol
  |       L1 Venting panel  →  L0 Aluminum sheet
  |
  +-- Option C: Static UPS + VRLA battery string (legacy, still installed)
  |     L2 Rectifier-inverter assembly  →  L1 IGBT module  →  L0 Si + Cu
  |     L2 VRLA battery string  →  L1 Lead-acid cell
  |         L0 Lead + H2SO4 + AGM separator
  |
  +-- Option D: STATCOM + Supercapacitor (fast sag mitigation, short duration)
  |     L2 Supercapacitor module  →  L1 EDLC cell
  |         L0 Activated carbon + acetonitrile electrolyte + Al collector
  |     L2 STATCOM PCS  →  L1 IGBT  →  L0 Si + Cu

L4 Main power distribution
  L3 MV transformer  →  L2 Core-coil assy  →  L1 Core  →  L0 Grain-oriented electrical steel
                                            →  L1 Winding  →  L0 Copper
                                            →  L1 Insulating fluid  →  L0 Mineral oil OR ester (FR3)
  L3 MV switchgear  →  L2 VCB cell  →  L1 Vacuum interrupter  →  L0 Cu-Cr contact alloy
                                     →  L1 Insulating gas  →  L0 SF6 OR dry air / fluoronitrile
L4 Power conditioning
  L3 Active harmonic filter  →  L2 IGBT module  →  L1 IGBT die  →  L0 Silicon
  L3 Reactive compensator (SVC/STATCOM)  →  L2 Thyristor valve  →  L1 Thyristor  →  L0 Silicon
L4 Control & monitoring
  L3 SCADA/PQ RTU  →  L2 Relay / IED  →  L1 PCBA  →  L0 FR4 + Si ICs
L4 Fire-protection & containment
  L3 Detection  →  L2 Aspirating smoke detector  →  L1 Tubing + sensor head  →  L0 PVC + Si sensor
  L3 Suppression  →  L2 Agent cylinder  →  L1 Cylinder  →  L0 Steel + NOVEC 1230
  L3 Deflagration management  →  L2 Pressure relief panel  →  L1 Panel  →  L0 Aluminum composite
```

### B.3 Alternatives at Key Level Transitions

**L4 Ride-through / energy reserve → L3 Ride-through asset (THE core choice point where Mein Solar competes)**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Flywheel UPS (dynamic) | 50% | → Stable | HIGH | [Spherical Insights Flywheel UPS 2026](https://www.sphericalinsights.com/blogs/top-20-companies-in-global-flywheel-ups-market-global-share-market-size-revenue-report-2026-2035); [Active Power fab use case](https://www.activepower.com/blog/active-power-flywheel-ups-power-back-up-is-vital-for-semiconductor-manufacturing/) |
| Static UPS + VRLA battery string | 25% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Schneider/Eaton fab references |
| Li-ion BESS (containerized, sag + bridging) | 12% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | [Schneider Electric fab PQ blog 2025](https://blog.se.com/sustainability/2025/07/04/semiconductor-fab-power-quality/); analyst estimate |
| STATCOM + supercapacitor (sag-only) | 8% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | ABB PCS100, analyst estimate |
| Ferroresonant transformer / DVR | 5% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | IEEE PES papers |

Shares sum 100%. Flywheel dominance in fabs driven by SEMI F47 profile (millisecond response, no thermal runaway risk inside cleanroom-adjacent facilities). **Mein Solar's BESS enters as the bridging/longer-duration asset**, often paired behind a flywheel front end.

**L3 Li-ion BESS → L2 Battery rack — Cell chemistry alternatives**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| LFP (LiFePO4) prismatic | 72% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | BloombergNEF, CATL/BYD shipments |
| NMC/NCA pouch or prismatic | 22% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | BNEF stationary report |
| LTO (lithium titanate) | 4% | → Stable | MEDIUM | niche for high C-rate/long life |
| Sodium-ion | 2% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | CATL/HiNa pilots, analyst estimate |

**L2 Battery cell → L0 Cathode material (inside LFP path)**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| LiFePO4 (standard) | 95% | → Stable | HIGH | single-source within LFP path |
| LMFP (manganese-doped) | 5% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Gotion/BYD 2025 pilots |

**L3 MV transformer → L1 Insulating fluid**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mineral oil | 70% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | IEEE/CIGRE surveys |
| Natural ester (FR3) | 20% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Cargill FR3 adoption, EU F-gas rules |
| Synthetic ester (Midel) | 8% | → Stable | MEDIUM | M&I Materials |
| Silicone fluid | 2% | → Stable | HIGH | niche indoor/fire-critical |

**L3 MV switchgear → L1 Insulating gas**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SF6 | 60% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | EU F-gas phase-down (Reg. 2024/573) |
| Dry air / vacuum | 25% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Siemens Blue GIS, ABB dry-air |
| Fluoronitrile (C4 / Novec 4710) | 15% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | GE g3, Hitachi EconiQ |

**L2 PCS → L1 Switching device (BESS inverter)**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Silicon IGBT | 80% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Infineon / Fuji reports |
| SiC MOSFET | 18% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Yole SiC 2025 |
| GaN HEMT | 2% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | low-power, not yet MW class |

**L2 Fire suppression → L1 Agent (for Li-ion container)**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NOVEC 1230 (FK-5-1-12) | 55% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | 3M exit announcement; single-source: false but supply tightening |
| Condensed aerosol (Stat-X, FirePro) | 25% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | UL 2775 references |
| Water mist | 15% | → Stable | MEDIUM | VdS/FM Global guidance |
| CO2 (legacy) | 5% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | phased out for occupied spaces |

### B.4 Component-Category Map — BESS Container (Mein Solar-scoped)

The BESS container appears once in BOM 2 (at L3 under ride-through). However, within BOM 2 Mein Solar's component technology could also appear at:

| Position in other fab subsystems | Shared tech | Adaptation |
|---|---|---|
| L4 Main power distribution → grid-edge storage for DSO deferral | PCS + container | Same MV coupling — identical |
| L4 Power conditioning → STATCOM with Li-ion energy buffer | PCS + cells | Smaller energy, different control — partial |
| Co-located PV roof → PV+BESS hybrid for fab self-supply | Full EPC scope | No adaptation — identical to Mein Solar reference |

---

sections.title: B.5 Company Position Marking
sections.content:
```
NAICS 334413 — Semiconductor Fab (VN L5 facility = fab site)
|
+-- VN System: Fab Power-Quality & Ride-Through System (BOM 2)
|   |
|   L4 Main power distribution
|   L4 Ride-through / energy reserve
|   |   |
|   |   L3 Ride-through asset
|   |       |
|   |       +-- L3 Li-ion BESS module       ← [MEIN SOLAR GmbH] position
|   |       |     (delivered as turnkey EPC container incl. L2 battery rack,
|   |       |      L2 PCS, L2 fire suppression, L2 thermal mgmt, L2 MV tie-in)
|   |       +-- L3 Flywheel UPS (competitor)
|   |       +-- L3 Static UPS + VRLA (competitor)
|   |       +-- L3 STATCOM + supercap (competitor)
|   |
|   L4 Power conditioning
|   L4 Control & monitoring
|   L4 Fire-protection & containment (envelope contribution from BESS container)
```

**Mein Solar role at this market:** L3 component supplier delivered through EPC wrapper. Mein Solar does NOT deliver the entire L4 ride-through subsystem (fab integrator / facility GC owns that), and does NOT touch BOM 1 (the chip itself).

---

sections.title: B.6 Strategic Adjacency
sections.content:
### Horizontal (at L4 inside Fab Power-Quality system)

| Adjacent L4 | Capability gap | Market size est. | Strategic fit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Main power distribution (MV transformer + switchgear) | MEDIUM (Mein Solar does MV tie-in inside EPC but not standalone) | Large, fragmented | WEAK — Siemens/ABB incumbency too strong |
| Power conditioning (active filters, STATCOM) | HIGH — requires power-electronics product development | Medium | WEAK |
| Control & monitoring (SCADA/PQ RTU) | HIGH — IT/OT software competence missing | Small | WEAK |
| Fire-protection & containment (full fab scope) | HIGH — specialty trade | Medium | WEAK |

### Vertical Upward (in BOM 2)

From L3 BESS module to L4 Ride-through subsystem integrator or L5 Fab Power System integrator.

| Factor | Evaluation |
|---|---|
| Additional capabilities required | Fab-grade PQ engineering (SEMI F47, ITIC, sag-source identification), cleanroom-adjacent civil, fab insurance class |
| Capex required | MEDIUM — process engineering hiring, not capital equipment |
| Customer relationship leverage | LOW — fab customers use specialty fab-GCs (e.g., Exyte, M+W Group) |
| Competitive response risk | HIGH — Exyte/Linesight own this layer |
| Time to market | 3–5 years (fab reference required) |

**Verdict:** BLOCKED without a fab reference. Entry strategy = subcontract under a fab GC, not direct sale.

### Vertical Downward / Cross-system

BESS container technology appears at L3 in many other fab-adjacent systems and in non-fab industries:

| Target system (fab) | BOM position | Shared tech | Adaptation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Data center hybrid UPS+BESS (NAICS 518210, adjacent market) | L3 battery module | Full container + PCS + EMS | Availability SLA, redundancy architecture |
| Fab PV rooftop / parking canopy BESS | L3 storage | Full EPC scope | None — identical to Mein Solar core |
| H2 electrolyzer firming (fab green-H2 PPA) | L3 storage | Full EPC scope | EMS co-optimization with electrolyzer |

**Strongest cross-system play:** bundle fab BESS with rooftop PV self-supply. This activates Mein Solar's PV+BESS differentiator AND gives the fab a complement (Scope 2 decarbonization) not served by Flywheel UPS incumbents.

---

sections.title: Quality Checks
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| BOM 1 end product produced | ✅ |
| BOM 2 VN-system BOM produced (Mein Solar position) | ✅ |
| Every L4 decomposes to L0 | ✅ (in BOM 2; BOM 1 compacted but with L0 identified on every branch) |
| Alternatives table at core choice points | ✅ (L3 ride-through asset; L2 cell chemistry; L0 cathode; L1 transformer fluid; L1 switchgear gas; L1 PCS semiconductor; L1 fire agent) |
| Shares sum 100% (±1%) | ✅ |
| Packaging/containment subsystem included | ✅ (fire-protection & containment L4) |
| Control/interface subsystem included | ✅ (control & monitoring L4) |
| Company position tagged | ✅ (L3 inside L4 Ride-through in BOM 2) |
| Horizontal + vertical adjacency done | ✅ |
| No brand names inside BOM itself | ✅ (brand names only in sourcing citations) |

---

Sources

L4 Interconnect > L3 Metal wiring > L1 Metal line
L4 Substrate > L3 Wafer > L0 Wafer material

l4_subsystems.function: Step-down from utility feeder to fab MV/LV buses

l4_subsystems.name: Ride-through / energy reserve
l4_subsystems.function: Hold fab tool load through SEMI F47 sags (50%/200 ms, 70%/500 ms, 80%/1 s) and short outages

alternatives.name: Flywheel UPS (dynamic)
alternatives.source: Spherical Insights Flywheel UPS 2026 report; Active Power fab application notes

alternatives.name: Li-ion BESS (containerized)

core_competitive_choice_point.notes: Flywheel dominance driven by SEMI F47 profile — millisecond response, no thermal runaway inside cleanroom-adjacent facilities. BESS grows as bridging/longer-duration layer, often paired behind a flywheel front end.

secondary_choice_points.position: L3 Li-ion BESS > L2 Battery rack > L1 Cell chemistry
L1 LFP cell > L0 Cathode material
L3 MV transformer > L1 Insulating fluid
L3 MV switchgear > L1 Insulating gas
L2 Fire suppression > L1 Suppression agent (Li-ion container)
